stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the Meta-Llama-3-8B model, a significant entry in the LLM landscape.
It is presented through the Hugging Face Benchmarks, indicating its community relevance and performance tracking.
The associated tags confirm its core technology stack involves transformers and text-generation capabilities.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the 'deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under text-generation, indicating its primary function as a large language model (LLM).
The associated tags suggest relevance to transformers, safetensors, and conversational AI applications.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model.
It is categorized under text-generation pipelines, indicating its function as a large language model.
The tags confirm its relevance to the broader transformer and Llama ecosystem in AI.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It indicates the model is being used for text generation tasks.
Associated technologies include transformers, PyTorch, and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance gains.
The integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing LLM execution on Apple hardware.
This is currently available in a preview build, signaling an ongoing optimization effort.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs entirely on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI assistance for coding tasks.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, both locally and in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is partnering with OpenAI and ROOST to integrate gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models.
These models are designed for safety classification tasks and are available in 20B and 120B sizes.
The gpt-oss-safeguard models are released under the permissive Apache 2.0 license.

stories.title: Meta’s Forum is part Reddit, part Facebook, and part Google AI Overview
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Meta is launching a new 'Forum' app to house Facebook Groups, consolidating functionality into a dedicated platform.
The app integrates a dedicated AI chatbot, positioning it as a competitor to tools like ChatGPT and Google's AI Overviews.
This move signals Meta's ongoing effort to modernize and centralize its community features, reviving a concept from a previously defunct Facebook Groups app.

stories.title: Four Russian satellites are now within striking distance of an ICEYE radarsat
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: Russian satellites are reportedly close enough to threaten an ICEYE radarsat.
The proximity suggests a capability beyond standard satellite operational profiles.
The story touches upon escalating geopolitical tensions involving space assets.

stories.title: MOSS: Self-Evolution through Source-Level Rewriting in Autonomous Agent Systems
stories.author: Yike Guo
bullet_points: MOSS proposes a method for self-evolution in autonomous agents by allowing source-level rewriting.
Current self-evolving agents are limited because they only modify text artifacts (prompts, schemas) and not the core agent code.
The research addresses the limitation of static agent harnesses by targeting code elements like routing, hook ordering, and dispatch.

stories.title: DeltaBox: Scaling Stateful AI Agents with Millisecond-Level Sandbox Checkpoint/Rollback
stories.author: Haibo Chen
bullet_points: Introduces DeltaBox, a system designed to address the latency bottleneck in stateful AI agents.
The core problem addressed is the slow (hundreds of milliseconds to seconds) checkpoint/rollback (C/R) of large sandbox states required for deep search and RL in LLM agents.
DeltaBox aims to enable millisecond-level C/R by optimizing state management, which is crucial for scaling complex AI agent workflows.

stories.title: MOSS: Self-Evolution through Source-Level Rewriting in Autonomous Agent Systems
stories.author: Yike Guo
bullet_points: Addresses the limitation of current autonomous agents being static post-deployment, requiring manual updates for failures.
Proposes 'MOSS,' a method for self-evolution that goes beyond modifying text-based artifacts (like prompts or skill files).
Focuses on evolving the core agent harness—including routing, hook ordering, and state invariants—which is currently untouched.

stories.title: Evaluating Commercial AI Chatbots as News Intermediaries
stories.author: James Zou
bullet_points: The study systematically evaluates six major commercial AI chatbots (including Gemini, GPT-5, Claude 4.5, etc.) regarding their factual accuracy.
It addresses a critical gap in research by measuring how these proprietary systems handle emerging news facts across different languages and regions.
The evaluation used 2,100 factual questions derived from same-day BBC News reporting over a two-week period.

stories.title: From Prompts to Action: What Gemini 3.5 Flash and the Agentic Stack Mean for Developers
stories.author: harshabm_e558522b28f940
bullet_points: The article discusses the implications of Gemini 3.5 Flash for software development.
It focuses on the concept of the 'Agentic Stack' and its relevance to modern development workflows.
The content is framed around key announcements from Google I/O.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now offers experimental local image generation capabilities on macOS.
This feature allows users to run image generation models entirely on their local machines.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local model deployment and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex, when integrated, can read, modify, and execute code within a user's directory.
The system supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Google’s AI search is so broken it can ‘disregard’ what you’re looking for
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Google's new AI Overviews feature is experiencing functional issues.
The AI has been observed providing irrelevant or chatbot-like responses to specific queries, such as 'disregard'.
This highlights current instability and limitations in the deployment of Google's generative AI search capabilities.

stories.title: SpaceX's Starship V3—still a work in progress—mostly successful on first flight
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: SpaceX's Starship V3 flight was deemed 'mostly successful' in its initial test.
The vehicle is still considered a work in progress and has significant hurdles to overcome.
Full operational capability, including reaching low-Earth orbit, has not yet been achieved.

stories.title: US scrambles to stop Internet users re-creating dead pilots’ voices
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: The story concerns the misuse of AI voice cloning technology to recreate the voices of deceased pilots.
This activity is happening despite existing regulations that restrict the disclosure of cockpit audio recordings by the NTSB.
The current situation involves users finding workarounds to bypass these legal restrictions.

stories.title: Vector Policy Optimization: Training for Diversity Improves Test-Time Search
stories.author: Pulkit Agrawal
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation in current Large Language Models (LLMs) where standard training optimizes for a single scalar reward.
It proposes 'Vector Policy Optimization' to enable LLMs to generate diverse response distributions, which is crucial for advanced inference-time search procedures like AlphaEvolve.
The research focuses on improving LLM generalization capabilities to handle novel environments and complex, multi-faceted reward functions during testing.

stories.title: The Matching Principle: A Geometric Theory of Loss Functions for Nuisance-Robust Representation Learning
stories.author: Vishal Rajput
bullet_points: Proposes a unified 'Matching Principle' to address various robustness challenges in representation learning.
The core idea is to estimate the covariance of 'label-preserving deployment nuisance' and regularize the encoder Jacobian based on it.
This framework aims to unify traditionally separate problems like domain adaptation, occlusion invariance, and temporal robustness.

stories.title: Gated DeltaNet-2: Decoupling Erase and Write in Linear Attention
stories.author: Jan Kautz
bullet_points: Introduces Gated DeltaNet-2, an advancement in linear attention mechanisms.
Addresses the challenge of editing compressed memory in linear attention models, moving beyond simple forgetting.
Utilizes Delta-rule models and Kimi Delta Attention (KDA) to sharpen forgetting and manage state updates.

stories.title: LCGuard: Latent Communication Guard for Safe KV Sharing in Multi-Agent Systems
stories.author: Karthikeyan Natesan Ramamurthy
bullet_points: The paper introduces LCGuard, a mechanism designed to ensure safe sharing of latent communication within multi-agent systems.
Latent communication via transformer Key-Value (KV) caches is noted as an efficient method for LLM coordination, but it poses privacy/security risks.
LCGuard addresses the opacity issue by guarding the sensitive, context-rich information encoded within the KV caches.

stories.title: MambaGaze: Bidirectional Mamba with Explicit Missing Data Modeling for Cognitive Load Assessment from Eye-Gaze Tracking Data
stories.author: John Quarles
bullet_points: MambaGaze addresses challenges in real-time cognitive load assessment from eye-gaze data.
It specifically tackles data missingness (from blinks/tracking failures) and long-range temporal dependencies.
The framework aims to enable adaptive human-centered AI in safety-critical applications like driver monitoring.

stories.title: CogAdapt: Transferring Clinical ECG Foundation Models to Wearable Cognitive Load Assessment via Lead Adaptation
stories.author: John Quarles
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of applying large clinical ECG foundation models to real-world wearable devices for cognitive load assessment.
Proposes 'CogAdapt,' a framework designed to adapt pre-trained clinical ECG models to the constraints of wearable sensors.
Focuses on improving cross-subject generalization and overcoming sensor configuration mismatches in cognitive monitoring.

stories.title: Vector Policy Optimization: Training for Diversity Improves Test-Time Search
stories.author: Pulkit Agrawal
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation in current Large Language Models (LLMs) where standard training optimizes for a single scalar reward, leading to low-entropy (non-diverse) outputs.
It proposes 'Vector Policy Optimization' to enable LLMs to generalize and perform better within inference-scaling search procedures (like AlphaEvolve).
The core contribution is improving the diversity of LLM responses, which is necessary for effective task-specific reward function selection during testing.

stories.title: The Matching Principle: A Geometric Theory of Loss Functions for Nuisance-Robust Representation Learning
stories.author: Vishal Rajput
bullet_points: Proposes a unified 'Matching Principle' to address various robustness issues in representation learning.
The core idea is to estimate the covariance of 'label-preserving deployment nuisance' and regularize the encoder Jacobian based on it.
This framework potentially unifies disparate problems like domain adaptation, photometric invariance, and temporal robustness.

stories.title: LCGuard: Latent Communication Guard for Safe KV Sharing in Multi-Agent Systems
stories.author: Karthikeyan Natesan Ramamurthy
bullet_points: The research addresses the security and privacy risks associated with sharing latent information (specifically KV caches) in multi-agent LLM systems.
It introduces 'LCGuard,' a mechanism designed to guard or sanitize the sensitive information encoded within transformer KV caches during inter-agent communication.
The work highlights the growing trend of using latent communication for efficiency in complex, LLM-based multi-agent coordination.

stories.title: FAME: Failure-Aware Mixture-of-Experts for Message-Level Log Anomaly Detection
stories.author: Alberto Leon-Garcia
bullet_points: Addresses the limitation of current log anomaly detectors which operate at coarse session/window levels.
Proposes FAME, a Failure-Aware Mixture-of-Experts model for fine-grained, message-level log anomaly detection.
Tackles challenges in message-level detection, such as template ambiguity and heterogeneous subsystem failures.

stories.title: Reducing Political Manipulation with Consistency Training
stories.author: Dan Hendrycks
bullet_points: LLMs display systematic and asymmetric political bias across sensitive contexts, termed 'covert political bias'.
The research identifies seven distinct categories of techniques through which this covert bias manifests.
Two new metrics, Sentiment Consistency and Helpfulness Consistency, are proposed to quantify this bias.

stories.title: Understanding Data Temporality Impact on Large Language Models Pre-training
stories.author: Grave Edouard
bullet_points: Investigates how data ordering during pre-training affects Large Language Models' ability to acquire time-sensitive factual knowledge.
Addresses the current limitation where LLMs, trained on shuffled data, have poorly understood temporal grounding.
Introduces a new benchmark comprising over 7,000 temporally grounded questions for evaluation.

stories.title: ChronoMedKG: A Temporally-Grounded Biomedical Knowledge Graph and Benchmark for Clinical Reasoning
stories.author: Richard Röttger
bullet_points: ChronoMedKG addresses a critical limitation in existing biomedical knowledge graphs (KGs) by incorporating temporal information.
The research highlights that static disease associations fail to capture the dynamic nature of clinical reasoning (e.g., age-dependent symptoms).
The new KG and benchmark aim to improve longitudinal clinical reasoning and retrieval augmentation in healthcare AI.

stories.title: AMEL: Accumulated Message Effects on LLM Judgments
stories.author: Sid-ali Temkit
bullet_points: Introduces the 'Accumulated Message Effect on LLM Judgments' (AMEL), investigating if prior conversation history biases LLM evaluations.
The study tested 11 LLMs from 4 major providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and open-source models) across 75,898 API calls.
Highlights a critical issue in using LLMs as automated evaluators (e.g., code review, content moderation) where context accumulation matters.

stories.title: The Control Plane is Leaking: When Context Becomes Command
stories.author: toxy4ny
bullet_points: The article discusses the blurring boundary between data and control when using Large Language Models (LLMs).
It addresses the concept of the 'Control Plane' leaking, suggesting a security or architectural risk.
The core theme is the need to re-establish separation mechanisms in LLM-driven systems.
